summary: Frontend-only verification of SL5 overnight split-sleeper scenario. All requested features are implemented and rendered correctly. Login via Badge 042 / PIN 1234 works. /hours-of-service/split-sleeper Learn tab renders 5 learn cards SL1-SL5. SL5 is a multi-day card with two EldGrids (Day 1 Mon + Day 2 Tue), 'Multi-day' badge, correct bracket annotations on both days, a 'continues overnight' hint between the grids, and a description citing §395.1(g)(1)(ii) with the reference rendered as a clickable cfr-link. SL1-SL4 regression intact (no Multi-day badge, no day sub-grids).

runtime_verification.login_badge042_pin1234: PASS (two-step: badge then PIN, Enter to submit)
runtime_verification.learn_tab_default_active: PASS — tab-learn pill highlighted gold; 5 learn cards visible
runtime_verification.SL5 Multi-day badge: PASS — rendered (CSS uppercase shows 'MULTI-DAY'; DOM text is 'Multi-day')

code_review_observations: hosScenarios.js (lines 852-893): SL5 entry is structured as multiDay:true with days:[{label,log,qualifyingBrackets,countedBrackets},...]. Math adds up — Day 1 counted = 6h + 5h = 11h toward 14; driving = 5h + 5h = 10h (within 11). 8h SB across midnight (19:00 Day1 -> 03:00 Day2) qualifies as Period A under §395.1(g)(1)(ii). New shift on Day 2 cleanly demarcated.

SplitSleeperPage.js LearnCard branch (lines 77-100) correctly detects s.multiDay and renders one EldGrid per day with combined (qualifying + counted) brackets, day labels in a subtle uppercase header, and a 'continues overnight ↓' hint on all days except the last. The non-multi-day branch (lines 101-112) is unchanged — SL1-SL4 continue to render identically.
CfrText converts §395.1 references into anchor tags with data-testid='cfr-link'. Verified 1 cfr-link inside the SL5 description paragraph (the §395.1(g)(1)(ii) citation is clickable).
critical_code_review_comments: All user-requested changes are implemented correctly and render end-to-end in the preview. No bugs or regressions detected. Note that 'Multi-day' and day labels render visually as uppercase due to CSS text-transform (intentional styling) — the raw DOM text remains as authored.
context_for_next_testing_agent: SL5 multi-day overnight scenario verified. Login is two-step: type badge then Enter, then type PIN then Enter. The 'Multi-day' pill and 'Day 1 · Mon' / 'Day 2 · Tue' labels are styled with CSS text-transform:uppercase — when asserting via page.inner_text() use case-insensitive comparison. Testids available: learn-card-SL1..SL5, learn-card-SL5-day-0, learn-card-SL5-day-1, cfr-link inside each description.
rca of the issue: N/A — additive feature, not a bug fix.
